hallucinations.description: The paper reports consistent supervised probe accuracy of 79.6% and specific NMI values across repositories, but the provided code logs and gate_evaluation.json reveal multiple failed runs (e.g., ValueError due to single-class data in train split), varying accuracies (0.867, 0.933), low kappa (0.115), and gate status FAIL with NMI ~0.023. Results appear fabricated or cherry-picked from inconsistent synthetic runs rather than reproducible experiments.
hallucinations.evidence: Code run_experiment.py logs show 'ValueError: This solver needs samples of at least 2 classes'; gate_evaluation.json has 'semantic': 0.0229, 'gate_status': 'FAIL'; paper claims 79.6% overall accuracy and repository-stratified NMI (UCI 0.394).

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: faked/misrepresented experimental results due to inconsistencies between paper claims and actual code execution outcomes (failed trainings, synthetic annotations, gate failures).
